steps.title: Combine like terms in $(6-7x)$
steps.content: Both sides contain copies of $(6-7x)$. On the left, $3(6-7x)+5(6-7x) = 8(6-7x)$, so the equation becomes $8(6-7x)+10 = 7(6-7x)+4$.

steps.title: Isolate $(6-7x)$
steps.content: Subtract $7(6-7x)$ from both sides: $(6-7x)+10 = 4$. Subtract $10$ from both sides: $(6-7x) = -6$.

steps.title: Convert to the target expression
steps.content: Since $7x-6 = -(6-7x)$, we have $7x-6 = -(-6) = 6$. The answer is 6.
